# sccfm_service_group (Data Source)

Use this data source to look up an existing service group by name.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the object to look up.

### Read-Only

- `description` (String) A human-readable description of the object.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the object. This is a UUID generated by the object service.
- `overrides` (Attributes List) List of per-target overrides. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--overrides))
- `referenced_object_uids` (Set of String) Set of UIDs of service objects referenced by this group.
- `values` (Attributes List) Inline service values included in the group. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--values))

<a id="nestedatt--overrides"></a>
### Nested Schema for `overrides`

Read-Only:

- `referenced_object_uids` (Set of String)
- `target_id` (String)
- `values` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--overrides--values))

<a id="nestedatt--overrides--values"></a>
### Nested Schema for `overrides.values`

Read-Only:

- `protocol` (String) The protocol (e.g., `TCP`, `UDP`, `ICMP`).
- `value` (String) The port value (e.g., `80`, `8000-8080`).



<a id="nestedatt--values"></a>
### Nested Schema for `values`

Read-Only:

- `protocol` (String) The protocol (e.g., `TCP`, `UDP`, `ICMP`).
- `value` (String) The port value (e.g., `80`, `8000-8080`).
